pageDescription: Calculate arrow speed, momentum, and kinetic energy using IBO ratings for archery performance analysis

definition.title: Arrow Speed Factors
definition.description: Arrow speed is affected by bow IBO rating, draw length, draw weight, and arrow weight

formula.title: Speed Calculation
formula.description: Adjusted Speed = IBO Speed - (3 × (Draw Weight - 70)) + (10 × (Draw Length - 30)) - (Arrow Weight - 400) ÷ 10

performance.title: Performance Metrics
performance.description: Momentum affects penetration, kinetic energy affects damage potential

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides estimates only and is not a substitute for actual measurement
criticalWarningsList.equipment: Actual performance varies with bow condition, archer form, and environmental factors

importantNotesList: Heavier arrows provide more momentum but lower speed
IBO ratings are measured with 70lb draw weight, 30" draw length, and 350gr arrow
Kinetic energy is more important for hunting than raw speed
Proper form and equipment maintenance affect actual performance

messages.enterInformation: Enter your bow and arrow specifications to calculate performance
messages.resultMessage: Your arrow performance calculations are shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides arrow performance estimates based on IBO standards and mathematical formulas. Actual performance may vary based on bow condition, archer form, and environmental factors.

seekHelp.title: Archery Resources
seekHelp.content: For professional archery analysis and equipment selection, consult certified archery instructors or use specialized chronographs for actual performance measurement.

errors.iboSpeed: Please enter a valid IBO speed
errors.drawLength: Please enter a valid draw length (20-35 inches)
errors.drawWeight: Please enter a valid draw weight (10-100 lbs)
errors.arrowWeight: Please enter a valid arrow weight (100-800 grains)
errors.additionalWeight: Please enter a valid additional weight

tooltips.iboSpeed: Manufacturer's IBO speed rating for your bow
tooltips.drawLength: Distance from nock point to pivot point plus 1.75 inches
tooltips.drawWeight: Force required to draw the bow at 28 inches
tooltips.arrowWeight: Total weight of arrow including point, shaft, and fletching
tooltips.additionalWeight: Weight of accessories like sights, rests, and stabilizers

enter_your_bow_and_arrow_specifications_: Enter your bow and arrow specifications to calculate performance metrics
based_on_30_draw_length_70_lb_draw_weigh:
Based on 30" draw length, 70 lb draw weight, and 350 grain arrow.
                        Adjustments: ±10 fps per inch of draw length, ±2 fps per pound of draw weight.
results_are_estimates_based_on_industrys: Results are estimates based on industry-standard IBO calculations.
enter_your_bow_specs_and_click_21: Enter your bow specs and click
ibo_international_bowhunting_organizatio:
IBO (International Bowhunting Organization) rating is a standardized measurement based on a
                        30-inch draw length, 70-pound draw weight, and 350-grain arrow. This provides a baseline for
                        comparing bow performance across different manufacturers.
draw_length_10_fps_per_inch_change_36: Draw length: ±10 fps per inch change
draw_weight_2_fps_per_pound_change_37: Draw weight: ±2 fps per pound change
draw_weight_60_lb_10_lb_from_baseline_44: Draw Weight: 60 lb (-10 lb from baseline)
arrow_weight_400_gr_50_gr_from_baseline_: Arrow Weight: 400 gr (+50 gr from baseline)
momentum_and_kinetic_energy_are_calculat: Momentum and kinetic energy are calculated using physics formulas: p = mv and KE = ½mv².
